items.question: For observed y = 1 from Bernoulli(p), what is L(p)?
items.explanation: Bernoulli PMF with y=1 fixed gives L(p) = p^1(1−p)^0 = p—likelihood as a function of p.

items.question: With 8 heads in 10 flips, which p maximizes L(p) = p^8(1−p)^2 among the choices?
For IID data, if ℓ(θ) = Σ log f(x_i | θ), how is L(θ) related to ℓ(θ)?
items.explanation: Log-likelihood is the logarithm of the product likelihood: ℓ(θ) = log L(θ), so L(θ) = exp(ℓ(θ)).

items.question: Which statement about likelihood is correct?
options: L(θ) must integrate to 1 over θ
L(θ) and f(x | θ) always use different formulas
L(θ) fixes data and varies θ; f(x | θ) typically fixes θ and varies x
L(θ) equals P(θ | x) without a prior
items.answer: L(θ) fixes data and varies θ; f(x | θ) typically fixes θ and varies x
items.explanation: Likelihood and probability share f(x | θ) but swap which quantity is fixed—the canonical distinction for this topic.

items.question: True class y = 2, softmax output π_2 = 0.25. What is the per-example negative log-likelihood?
items.explanation: Categorical NLL for the true class is −log π_y = −log(0.25) ≈ 1.386.
